faqs.question: कहानी 'दोस्त की पोशाक' का मुख्य संदेश क्या है?
faqs.answer: The main message of the story is that we should never judge people by their clothes or outward appearance. A person's true worth lies in their character, qualities (गुण), and behaviour, not in what they wear. The story shows how the same person was treated with disrespect in simple clothes and with honour in fancy clothes - proving that people were respecting the clothes, not the person.

faqs.question: दोस्त को पहली बार दावत में कैसे treat किया गया?
faqs.answer: When the friend came to the feast wearing simple, old clothes (साधारण कपड़े), he was not treated well. The people at the feast did not give him respect (सम्मान). They ignored him, gave him a poor seat far from the main table, and did not serve him food properly. This is because they judged him by his clothes and thought he was not important.

faqs.question: 'पोशाक' और 'कपड़े' में क्या अंतर है?
faqs.answer: Both words refer to clothing, but there is a slight difference. कपड़े (kapde) is the everyday, common word for clothes. पोशाक (poshak) is a more formal or literary word that often refers to a complete outfit or costume. For example, you might say 'school ke kapde' (school clothes) in daily speech, but 'raja ki poshak' (the king's costume) when talking about a specific outfit. In the story, पोशाक emphasises how the clothes became the focus of attention.

faqs.question: दोस्त ने कपड़ों पर खाना क्यों रखा?
faqs.answer: The friend put food on his clothes to make a powerful point! He wanted to show everyone that they were not respecting HIM - they were respecting his CLOTHES. By saying 'Let my clothes eat since you are honouring them, not me,' he cleverly exposed their shallow behaviour. It was his way of teaching them that real respect should be for the person, not for what they are wearing.

faqs.question: सच्ची दोस्ती और झूठी दोस्ती में क्या अंतर है?
faqs.answer: सच्ची दोस्ती (True friendship) means loving and respecting your friend no matter what - whether they wear simple clothes or fancy ones, whether they are rich or poor. True friends care about your heart, not your appearance. झूठी दोस्ती (False friendship) is based on outward things like money, looks, and status. False friends change their behaviour when your circumstances change. This story shows the difference clearly.

faqs.question: 'सम्मान' और 'अपमान' शब्दों का मतलब क्या है?
faqs.answer: सम्मान (Sammaan) means respect or honour. The prefix 'सम' means good or equal, and 'मान' means regard. So sammaan means treating someone with equal regard and dignity. अपमान (Apmaan) means disrespect or insult. The prefix 'अप' means bad or away. So apmaan means taking away someone's dignity. These are विलोम शब्द (opposite words). Example: हमें सबका सम्मान करना चाहिए, किसी का अपमान नहीं।

faqs.question: 'दिखावा' से क्या नुकसान होता है?
faqs.answer: Dikhava (दिखावा) or showing off and judging by appearances causes many problems: (1) It hurts people who are judged unfairly because of how they look. (2) It stops us from seeing someone's true qualities (गुण). (3) It creates false friendships based on appearances, not real connection. (4) It teaches wrong values to children. The story warns us that दिखावा leads to missing out on knowing truly good people.

faqs.question: कहानी में विलोम शब्द (opposite words) कौन-कौन से हैं?
faqs.answer: The story contains several important opposite word pairs: सम्मान (respect) ↔ अपमान (disrespect), सादा (simple) ↔ भड़कीला (flashy), सच्चा (true) ↔ नकली (fake), अच्छा (good) ↔ बुरा (bad), अमीर (rich) ↔ गरीब (poor), अंदर (inside) ↔ बाहर (outside). Learning opposites helps you understand both the story and expand your Hindi vocabulary!

faqs.question: 'अतिथि देवो भव' का क्या मतलब है?
faqs.answer: 'Atithi Devo Bhava' (अतिथि देवो भव) is an ancient Indian saying meaning 'The guest is like God.' It teaches that every guest who comes to your home should be treated with utmost respect and care, regardless of their appearance, clothes, or status. In this story, the people broke this value by treating their guest differently based on his clothes. True Indian culture says every guest deserves equal love and hospitality.

faqs.question: इस कहानी से मिलती-जुलती और कौन-सी कहानियाँ हैं?
faqs.answer: There are many stories with similar themes! In Hindi literature, there are tales like 'Birbal ki Khichdi' where appearances and status are questioned. The famous folk tale of Akbar and Birbal also teaches about judging people fairly. In English, the story 'The Emperor's New Clothes' deals with similar themes of appearance vs. reality. Even the Hindi saying 'सूरत से नहीं, सीरत से पहचानो' (Know people by their character, not their face) carries the same message.
